title: S3 Ep2: The Road to Cheltenham with Nico de Boinville
summary: It's Cheltenham week! And who better to join Pippa and Tristan this special week than one of jump racing’s most successful riders, Nico de Boinville. As the principal jockey for Champion Trainer Nicky Henderson, Nico has ridden more than 800 winners, claimed over 50 Grade 1 victories and celebrated many wins at the legendary Cheltenham Festival - including on Old Park Star this week - winning the Supreme Novices' Hurdle, the opening race of the 2026 Cheltenham Festival.  With the Cheltenham roar ringing out this week, Nico joins the pod to talk about the thrill, pressure and preparation behind the biggest meeting in jump racing. He reflects on his journey into the sport leaving university early to pursue a career in racing and working his way up. Nico also opens up about bouncing back after a recent fall earlier this season, the mindset it takes to recover from injury and get straight back in the saddle, and what it’s like riding some of the greatest horses in modern racing, including the phenomenal Constitution Hill. Plus and unusual technique to avoid post race interviews...  Plus we'll have a guest appearance from pod alumni Mick Fitzgerald and a mini Cheltenham quiz.  Keep sharing your stories, join us on social media, or drop us an email at info@talk-horse.com. See you soon for lots more Talk Horse.  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: S3 Ep1: Joe Ashman on Free Rein and Finding a Passion
summary: We’re back for Series 3 of Talk Horse! Did you miss us? We’re kicking things off in style with a guest hot off a flight from LA. Actor Joe Ashman, otherwise known as Callum in Netflix’s epic equine drama Free Rein, sits down with Olympic eventer Pippa Funnell and Made in Chelsea star Tristan Phipps to discuss all things horse. How he exaggerated his riding ability to land his dream role, why Free Rein ignited a love of horses, his bond with his on screen equine partner Milo, and his passion for the work he now does as an ambassador for the Riding for the Disabled Association. Donate to Joe's London Marathon for the RDA here https://2026tcslondonmarathon.enthuse.com/pf/joe-ashman?utm_source=ig&utm_medium=social&utm_content=link_in_bio&fbclid=PAZXh0bgNhZW0CMTEAc3J0YwZhcHBfaWQPMTI0MDI0NTc0Mjg3NDE0AAGnAzfYunM4mViLZVqiYGEXcUZp1-XfNuzgKAFcOAGKpGZpPJrNEiNdAeO8RiI_aem_-zt3qRICJM8d_PzMeo4IbQ Plus, he has an important message for Tristan about why it’s never too late to start riding. And the games are back! Today Pippa takes on Tristan in “Nay-Listers”. There may even have been some cheeky cheating. We’ll be revealing all on our socials this week... Whether you’re a lifelong rider or simply horse curious, saddle up and let’s Talk Horse.  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: S2 Ep19: Boyd Exell - Four Horses. One Man. Pure Excellence
summary: In the last of our live specials, Pippa and Tristan speak to the incredible Boyd Exell. Our first guest from the world of carriage driving and what a man to start with. One of the most decorated four-in-hand carriage drivers in history, Boyd has been crowned World Champion seven times in combined driving and has won the FEI World Cup Driving Final an unprecedented 11 times, more than any other driver. In a journey that took him from rural New South Wales to the very top of international carriage driving, Boyd reflects on how he first fell for the sport, the bold move to Europe that transformed his career, and the mindset required to stay at world-champion level for decades. He shares how he builds trust across a four-horse team, what continues to drive him after so many historic wins, and what goes through your mind when you drop the reins mid-race. Spoiler… he will not be doing that again. We’ll be back with series three from 3 March. Keep sharing your stories, join us on social media, or drop us an email at info@talk-horse.com. See you soon for lots more Talk Horse.  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: S2 Ep18: John, Donald and Jack Whitaker - Born to Jump
summary: In this special episode, recorded live on stage at the London International Horse Show, Pippa Funnell and Tristan Phipps are joined by three members of one of the most iconic families in showjumping, the Whitakers. John Whitaker is one of our most requested guests and you can see why. With a career spanning more than 40 years, he’s won everything there is to win, including three Olympic medals, five World Championship medals and thirteen European Championship medals. He chats about longevity at the top of the sport, the mindset required to keep improving, and how showjumping has evolved over the decades. Plus, what keeps him competing at 70 years old. Helping make sense of the family tree are Donald Whitaker, a key contributor to Britain’s 2025 European team silver and historic Nations Cup victory, and Jack Whitaker, a top competitor at senior level in Nations Cups, World Cup classes and the Global Champions Tour. If you’ve always wanted to know what Christmas is like for the Whitaker family, their tips for spooky horses, or what they eat for breakfast, this is the one for you. Saddle up and let’s Talk Horse. 🐴  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: S2 Ep16: Laura Kraut Olympic Showjumping Queen
summary: We're feeling festive in the studio and we hope you are too! In the last episode before our Christmas break Pippa and Tristan are joined by one of the most successful and enduring figures in international showjumping, Laura K***t. Representing the USA across multiple Olympics, Laura is a team gold medallist from Beijing 2008 and team silver medallist from Tokyo 2021 and Paris 2024. This also marks a first for Talk Horse, with Laura joining remotely! It's the future.  Laura shares her early connection with horses, her route into elite showjumping and the key moments that told her she could compete at the very top. She reflects on her decision to base herself in Europe, how that shaped her career, and what she has learned from preparing for multiple Olympic Games across decades. The conversation also dives into horsemanship, longevity in the sport, motivation, and what still drives her to improve. Plus, the hosts catch up on what is coming up at London International Horse Show, look ahead to this weeks live episodes and Tristan faces an end of year quiz that asks how much he has really learned about horses. Though our present to him is the questions were a bit too easy...  Have a wonderful Christmas break and we'll be back in 2026 to Talk more Horse with you!   Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: S2 Ep15: Lee Middleton - the Man Behind Equine America
summary: Get ready for a festive, feel-good episode of Talk Horse as Pippa Funnell and Tristan Phipps team up with Equine America and welcome long-time friend and industry powerhouse Lee Middleton. Lee’s journey takes us from navigating school with dyslexia to becoming a horse dentist, popularising innovative air safety jackets and eventually leading one of the biggest names in equine supplements. He shares the twists, the graft and the lessons that shaped his career, plus the products he rates most for horses, dogs and even humans. For his exclusive giveaway, head to https://www.cortaflex.co.uk/talkhorse.  There’s plenty of seasonal cheer too, with a chaotic round of 12 Days of Horsemas, live show updates for London International, and the usual mix of stories, laughs and behind-the-scenes equestrian insight.  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: S2 Ep14: Lydia Haywood Riding for Change
summary: Pippa Funnell and Tristan Phipps are joined by the incredible Lydia Haywood, the event rider shaking up the equestrian world. She’s the force behind Cool Ridings, a platform opening doors for new talent and championing inclusion across the sport. Demystifying the equine world by sharing knowledge and embracing horse lovers from underrepresented backgrounds.  Representing Jamaica, mentoring young riders and working as an ambassador for London International, Lydia brings passion, purpose and a seriously impressive work ethic. She shares her journey into horses, the moment she realised this was more than a hobby, and how she balances a full career with competing at a high level. And speaking of London International... title: S2 EP 13: From Hogwarts to Horsepower: Greg Powell Horsemaster and Stunt Man
summary: Riding into the studio this week is legendary stunt co-ordinator and movie horse master Greg Powell, the man behind some of the most iconic action moments in film.  If you’ve ever watched a high-speed horseback chase or a galloping escape in Harry Potter, Bond or Fast and Furious, chances are Greg had a hand in making it happen. Greg shares how he went from early days of riding horse and carts round south London, to choreographing scenes watched by millions. He talks film horses, on-set chaos, magical moments that nearly didn’t go to plan and the secrets behind training both actors and equines for heart-stopping stunts. . Plus Pippa and Tristan try out their new game Who Neighs the Plot... title: S2 Ep 12: Marina Fogle Takes the Reins
summary: Marina Fogle rides into Talk Horse for a chat with Pippa Funnell and Tristan Phipps, diving into everything from pony-mad childhood memories to rediscovering the joy of riding as an adult. She reveals how horses have become part of family life in the countryside, why her kids keep her firmly in Pony Club mum territory, and whether her adventurer husband Ben has ever been tempted to swap expeditions for stirrups. They swap stories about Burghley magic, laugh about the chaos and calm that horses bring, and compare notes on parenting, podcasting and outdoor life. Plus Pippa and Tristan have delved into our bulging mailbag to answer your questions and play a high stakes game of photo finish... title: S2 Ep 11: Oli Bell - Hitting His Stride
summary: Pippa Funnell and Tristan Phipps are joined by ITV Racing’s Oli Bell, one of the most recognisable faces in British horse racing. From his early start as a teenage reporter to fronting coverage of the Grand National, Oli shares the highs, the chaos, plus the occasional royal run-in. He opens up about growing up in a famous racing family, what it’s really like behind the scenes on race day, and how the conversation around equine welfare is shaping the sport’s future. Plus, Pippa and Tristan tackle your questions and face off in a frantic round of Photo Finish, and let’s just say, Pippa’s competitive streak is out in full force. title: S2 Ep 10: Becky Moody - Dressage Bombshell
summary: After last week’s shenanigans, Pippa Funnell and Tristan Phipps are firmly back in the equine world. They’re joined by dressage star and Olympic bronze medallist Becky Moody, the Yorkshire-born rider known for her effervescent personality, fabulous taste in freestyle music and brilliantly quirky horse names. Becky reflects on her late call-up from reserve to team member at the Paris Olympics and the whirlwind that followed, from the pressure of stepping onto the biggest stage to celebrating that unforgettable team bronze. She also shares the story of Jägerbomb, the homebred star she produced herself, and what it takes to build a partnership that shines under the spotlight. Plus, Tristan shows off an uncanny knack for tacking up small horses and Pippa fills us in on all the latest from the horse world. title: S2 Ep9: Miles Nazaire - Made in Chelsea's Stallion
summary: It's all a bit different this episode as it's Pippa Funnell's birthday, and everyone is very giddy. Luckily Tristan has bought her a special gift in the shape of “the good looking one” from Made in Chelsea - Miles Nazaire. Miles first became known for appearing alongside Tristan in Made in Chelsea, but he’s also been runner up on Dancing on Ice, is a passionate fitness influencer and ready to turn his hand to learning anything - including how to ride for an American reality series. Miles chats about his riding history, dating history and dishes the dirt on Tristan before everyone gets unexpectedly philosophical about the pressures life can throw at you and the paths life can take. But, don't worry - there's still plenty of elite horse content courtesy of Olympic horsewoman Pippa Funnell. title: S2 Ep7: Megan Elphick: Going Clear and Going Viral
summary: Pippa Funnell and Tristan Phipps are joined by eventer and social media star Megan Elphick, the rider behind Elphick Event Ponies, who has turned her grassroots eventing journey into a true digital phenomenon. From early days at Pony Club to producing her own horses and sharing every high (and the occasional bump back down to earth) with hundreds of thousands of followers, Megan has built a passionate community who love her honesty, humour and genuine love for horses. Pippa and Tristan chat to Megan about balancing eventing and content creation, what life is really like as an eventing influencer, and how social media is reshaping the equestrian world. Plus, Megan gives Pippa a crash course in dating apps, and Tristan tests her Gen Z slang knowledge. Will she truly embody “Mane Character Energy”? title: S2 Ep6: David Broome: Britain’s Showjumping Icon
summary: Pippa and Tristan sit down with one of the true icons of British showjumping, David Broome CBE. A six-time Olympian, three-time Olympic medalist, and World Champion, David’s remarkable career spanned decades and helped shape the golden age of British equestrian sport. He opens up about his unforgettable partnership with Mr Softee, the horse that made him a household name, and the grit, glamour, and occasional chaos of competing around the world when British showjumping was at its peak. From early memories of growing up in Wales and building a career alongside his father, to his reflections on how the sport has evolved and what still drives his passion today, it’s a rare and personal insight from one of the greats. title: S2 Ep5: William Fox-Pitt - Lessons from a Legend
summary: Pippa and Tristan are joined in the studio by one of the tallest, and most decorated, figures in Eventing history, William Fox-Pitt. From his record-breaking string of five-star wins at Badminton, Burghley, and Kentucky, to flying the flag for Britain at Olympic Games and World Championships, William’s career has set the standard for generations of riders. To add to his accolades - he's one of the most requested guests on Talk Horse.   We chat about his greatest horses, the highs and lows of life at the very top, and what it takes to stay at the sharp end of the sport for so long including his life changing accident in 2015, just before the Rio Olympics. Plus, Pippa relives some good times with her old friend, and Tristan delves into Pippa's own spectacular achievements.   Please do keep telling all your horsey friends about us. title: S2 Ep1: Hashtags and Horsepower with Harlow White and Demir
summary: Pippa Funnell and Tristan Phipps are back for Talk Horse Series Two. And we're off to a flying start as we welcome two of the biggest names in equestrian social media: Harlow White and Demir. Making her first ever podcast appearance, Harlow White has become one of the most recognisable young equestrian influencers online. With over one million followers across YouTube, TikTok and Instagram, her vlogs, pony life updates and riding challenges have built a passionate global fanbase of horse-mad followers. And we're lucky enough to also have Demir in the studio. His slick, creative and often hilarious equestrian content has exploded on TikTok and Instagram. His videos have introduced horse culture to new audiences, sparking conversations, laughs and plenty of viral moments.  Together in the Talk Horse studio, Harlow and Demir share their love of the equine world, talk about their social media journeys, and reveal what it takes to inspire and entertain such massive online communities. Plus pod husband and wife are finally reunited - one’s been back on the horse, the other’s been perfecting the art of the long holiday. We’ll let you guess who’s who...   We LOVE hearing from you. And please do keep telling all your horsey friends about us.   Email: info@talk-horse.com Instagram: @talkhorseofficial TikTok: @talkhorseofficial YouTube: @TalkHorse-Official Facebook: @talk-horse  Hosted on Acast. title: Special 4: Jodie Kidd Unbridled  - from Reins to Runways
summary: Welcome to the last of our summer specials, recorded on the morning of Derby Day in June 2025, right in the heart of Hickstead, Pippa and Tristan saddle up with supermodel, showjumper and speed enthusiast Jodie Kidd. A true polymath, Jodie has strutted catwalks, flown over showjumps, played polo at the highest levels, taken the chequered flag in fast cars and, as a board member at Hickstead, helped steer one of the most famous showgrounds in the world. In this episode, she opens the stable door on her many lives — from fashion shoots to cross-country courses — and reveals a few hidden passions you might not expect. It’s a ride that’s part glamour, part horsepower, and all Jodie Kidd. Watch this space as we'll be returning at the start of September with an incredible guest list. We can't wait to be back with you every week. We LOVE hearing from you.   Email: info@talk-horse.com Instagram: @talkhorseofficial TikTok: @talkhorseofficial YouTube: @TalkHorse-Official Facebook: @talk-horse    Hosted on Acast. title: Special 3: Shane Breen Jumping Machine!
summary: Recorded live on the morning of Derby Day, this special episode of Talk Horse with Pippa and Tristan features showjumper and breeder Shane Breen, speaking to us from his own front room, right beside the famous Hickstead arena. We chat with Shane just hours before the Al Shira'aa Hickstead Derby to explore what it means to compete on home ground, the bumpy path to coupling up with Chloe Bunn (daughter of Hickstead founder Douglas Bunn), and his progression from Pony European silver medallist to member of the Senior Irish National Team. Over the years, Shane has built an impressive international career, with Grand Prix victories at Lummen, Monaco and Dublin in 2019. That journey reached a major milestone in 2022, when he finally won the Hickstead Derby on Can Ya Makan, a triumph that came on his 17th attempt. In this episode, Shane discusses the pride and pressure of jumping at home, the strategy behind Derby Day, and what it takes to sustain a top-level career in showjumping, all while raising a family at Hickstead. We LOVE hearing from you.   Email: info@talk-horse.com Instagram: @talkhorseofficial TikTok: @talkhorseofficial YouTube: @TalkHorse-Official Facebook: @talk-horse    Hosted on Acast. title: Special 2: Running the Show - Inside Hickstead with Lizzie Bunn
summary: Welcome back to the second of our Talk Horse summer specials.  Recorded live at Hickstead on the morning of Derby Day, this episode takes you right to the beating heart of British showjumping. We’re joined by Lizzie Bunn, Show Director of the Al Shira’aa Hickstead Derby Meeting and Managing Director of the All England Jumping Course, for a rare and revealing conversation.   From the eye-watering sums paid for top international horses, to the complicated history of the Bunn family, Lizzie lifts the lid on what really goes into keeping this world-famous showground galloping. We talk about the intense build-up to Derby Day, the pressure, the pageantry and the fun behind the scenes.   She’s also best friends with Pippa Funnell… So expect some fishy tails from their long friendship. It’s emotional, funny, and full of the kind of insider detail only Lizzie can share.   A must-listen for anyone who’s ever dreamed of clearing the Derby Bank or just wondered what it takes to run the most iconic arena in the country. We LOVE hearing from you.   Email: info@talk-horse.com Instagram: @talkhorseofficial TikTok: @talkhorseofficial YouTube: @TalkHorse-Official Facebook: @talk-horse    Hosted on Acast. title: Special 1: Saddles, Sequins and Staying Power with Angela Rippon
summary: Talk Horse proper will be back soon. But while we brew that up, enjoy the first of our special episodes, recorded earlier this summer, where we sit down with broadcasting legend Angela Rippon to explore her lifelong love of horses. Whether you know her from Strictly Come Dancing or the Nine O’Clock News, this is yet another fascinating side to the incredible Angela.   From her early passion for horses to training as a jockey and even writing the book on it, Angela shares how equestrian life has shaped her world alongside a remarkable career in television. Expect riding adventures, heartfelt stories and reflections on how horses have kept her grounded both on and off screen. Angela also lets us in on her Strictly secrets and her ongoing work with animal welfare charities. Plus, Pippa and Tristan explore the very important question… how do you name a horse? title: Ep 26: The Rest is Horses
summary: BEST BITS COMPILATION THREE Whatever the story, the horse is at the heart.   In the last of our best bits, we’re celebrating the moments that show just how deeply horses shape lives. From the first spark that gets you into riding, to the thrill (and chaos) of celebrities learning to ride, and the grit it takes to get back in the saddle after a serious fall, these stories are packed with passion, humour, and heart.   Whether you’re a lifelong rider or just horse-curious, this episode is a love letter to the bond that keeps us all coming back to the stable.   Justine Ward – Event Rider and YouTube Star Sam Woolfenden – Farrier and Social Media Sensation Laura Collett – Olympic Gold Medallist Richard Davidson – Olympic Dressage Rider Marcus Capel – Pony Club CEO Adam Short – Top Level Groom Nigel Hollins – Showing Legend Chris Williams – Equine Dentist Hazel Jackson – World No 1 Women Polo Player Dan Naprous – Hollywood Horseman    Hosted on Acast. title: Ep 25: Getting Back in the Saddle
summary: BEST BITS COMPILATION TWO   Life’s twists, turns, and the horses that carry us through   We’re taking a short break, but don’t worry, we’re not leaving you without your horsey fix! We’ve bought together some of our favourite moments in a series of best of compilations.   Whether you're mucking out, hacking out, or just hanging out, these episodes are here to keep you company.   In this one, we’re diving into the deep, sometimes wild, and always wonderful love we all share for horses and the surprising, hilarious, and downright crazy places that passion can lead us. From unexpected career turns to meeting your life partner, hear the stories that prove once horses are in your blood, there’s no going back.   In this episode   Sam Woolfenden – Farrier and Social Media Sensation William Funnell – Champion Showjumper and Pippa’s other half Bob Champion – Grand National Winner Justine Ward – Event Rider and Social Media Queen Tullis Matson – Equine AI Expert Nigel Hollins – Showing Legend Tina Cook – Olympic Eventer Adam Short – Top Level Groom Rosie Tapner – Equine Reporter and ex Model Louise Bell – 5* Dressage Rider We LOVE hearing from you. title: Ep 24: Leading the Field - How to Reach the Top and Stay There

title: Ep 23: Double Trouble with Jack Whitaker and Joe Stockdale - Elite Showjumpers
summary: They’ve been on our dream guest list from day one, so Pippa and Tristan were over the moon to welcome showjumping sensations Jack Whitaker and Joe Stockdale to the Talk Horse Live Tent at the Royal Windsor Horse Show.   Rising stars of British showjumping, Jack Whitaker and Joe Stockdale compete at 5* level earning podium finishes and acclaim. They reflect on their shared equestrian heritage, the friendly yet fiercely competitive rivalry and the realities of life on the international showjumping circuit. They also let us in on some more unexpected sides of themselves, from romantic getaways and Joe’s domestic prowess to Jack’s unexpected turn as a Tatler model. It's the last show in a brilliant Series One of Talk Horse. Thanks for listening and we'll be back in a few weeks Talking Horse with more incredible guests. title: Ep 22: Blooming Legend Alan Titchmarsh
summary: It’s a really special episode this week. Not only are Pippa and Tristan coming to you live from the Royal Windsor Horse Show, but we’re breaking the old rule that says: never meet your heroes, because this week, Tristan gets to do exactly that!   Our guest is the fabulous Alan Titchmarsh - legendary presenter of all the biggest gardening shows, author of countless books, a true National Treasure, and someone with a deep love for horses, the great outdoors, and the Royal Windsor Horse Show itself. And Tristan is his biggest fan.   Alan swaps gardening tips with Pippa, shares riding tales with Tristan, and even tells us what befell his Madam Tussauds waxwork.
